explanations.1: This function takes a number (Byte type, which is 0-255) and returns it as a text string of 1s and 0s showing its binary representation.
explanations.10: The loop counts down from 7 to 0 because a byte has 8 bits numbered that way, with 7 being the leftmost (most important) bit.
explanations.11: This line checks if a specific bit position in the number is 'on' by using bit shifting (1 shl I makes a 1 jump I positions left) and the 'and' operation (which checks if both sides have a 1 in that spot).
